para.as_written: ܫܘܒܚܐ ܠܐܒܐ ܘܠܒܪܐ ܘܠܪܘܚܐ ܕܩܘܕܫܐ ܕܚܝܠ ܘܥܕܪ ܠܥܒܕܗ ܒܨܝܪܐ ܘܚܠܫܐ ܝܘܚܢܢ ܘܟܬܒ ܦܢܩܝܬܐ ܗܕܐ ܠܝܘܬܪܢܐ ܕܢܦܫܗ ܘܕܐܚܘ̈ܗܝ ܐܢܕܪܝܐ ܘܕܟܠ ܡܢܘ ܕܩ̇ܪܐ ܒܗ ܕܡܪܢ ܝܫܘܥ ܡܫܝܚܐ ܒܪܐ ܝܚܝܕܝܐ ܗ̇ܘ ܕܐܝܬܘܗܝ ܥܡ ܐܒܐ ܕܠܐ ܫܘܪܝ ܒܐܠܗܘܬܗ ܒܙܒ̈ܢܐ ܕܝܢ ܐܚܪ̈ܝܐ ܨܒ̣ܐ ܕܢܗܘܐ ܬܚܝܬ ܫܘܪܝܐ ܒܐܢܫܘܬܗ ܫܩ̣ܠ ܕܡܘܬܐ ܕܥܒܕܐ ܘܒܟܘܠ ܡܕܡ ܐܬܕܡܝ ܠܢ ܣܛܪ ܡܢ ܚܛܝܬܐ ܗ̣ܘ ܕܝܢ ܡܪܢ ܝܫܘܥ ܡܫܝܚܐ ܐܠܗܐ ܕܥܠ ܟܠ ܢܬܠ ܠܗ ܠܚܛܝܬܐ ܝܘܚܢܢ ܚܒܝܫܝܐ ܕܒܝܬ ܡܪܝ ܩܢܘܢ ܩܕܝܫܐ ܕܡܥܪܬ ܡܨܪܝܢ ܡܕܝܢܬܐ ܘܠܐܚܘ̈ܗܝ [ܘܟܠܗ ܥܡܐtwo words erased, perhaps] ܡܢܬܐ ܘܝܪܬܘܬܐ ܒܥܠܡܐ ܕܠܐ ܥ̇ܒܪ ܥܡ ܟܠܗܘܢ ܩܕܝ̈ܫܐ ܕܫܦ̣ܪ ܠܨܒܝܢܗ ܟܕ ܡܫܘܐ ܠܗܘܢ ܡܫܝܚܐ ܐܠܗܐ ܠܚܘܣܝܐ ܕܚܘ̈ܒܐ ܘܠܫܘܒܩܢܐ ܕܚܛܗ̈ܐ ܘܠܚܝ̈ܐ ܕܠܥܠܡ ܒܨܠܘܬܐ ܕܢܒܝ̈ܐ ܘܕܫܠܝ̈ܚܐ ܘܣܗ̈ܕܐ ܘܡܘܕܝ̈ܢܐ ܘܡܪܬܝ ܡܪܝܡ ܝܠܕܬ ܐܠܗܐ ܐܢ ܘܐܡܝܢ ܘܐܡܝܢ
translation: Glory to the Father, and to the Son, and to the Holy Spirit, who has strengthened and helped His wanting and feeble servant John; and he has written this volume, for the benefit of himself, and of his brethren, Andrew, and of every one who reads in it so that our Lord Jesus Christ, the Only Son, He who is with the Father without beginning in His Godhead, but who in the latter times has willed to become subject to a beginning in His manhood, took the form of a servant, and in everything became similar to us, except in sin. Now may He, our Lord Jesus Christ, God over all, give to the sinner, John the Recluse of Beth Mar QNWN the saint, in the city of Maʿarrat Meṣrēn, and to his brethren, and to [all the people], a part and an inheritance in the world that does not pass away ; with all the saints who pleased His will; making them worthy, God the Christ, for the remission of faults, and the forgiveness of sins, and the everlasting life, by the prayers of the prophets, and of the apostles, and the martyrs, and the confessors, and of the Lady Mary, the mother of God, yea, and Amen, and Amen.

assoc_name.value: John the Recluse of Beth Mar

note: Reproduced in A. Smith Lewis, Select Narratives of Holy Women from the Syro-Antiochene or Sinai Palimpsest (Studia Sinaitica IX; London, 1900), p. xxiii–xxiv

para.as_written: ܐܢܐ ܒܨܝܪ[ܐ] ܘܚܛܝܐ ܝܘܚܢܢ ܐܣܛܘܢܝܐ ܕܒܝܬ / ܡܪܝ ܩܢܘܢ ܩܕܝܫܐ ܕܡܥܪܬ ܡܨܪܝܢ ܡܕܝܢܬܐ ܟܘܪܐ / ܕܐܢܛܝܘܟܝܐ ܡ‍[ܕܝܢ‍]‍ܬ ܐܠܗܐ ܟܬܒܬ ܟܬܒܐ ܗܢܐ / ܐܝܟ ܕܠܝܘܬܪܢܐ ܕܢܦܫܝ ܘܕܐܚ̈ܝ ܘܕܐܝܠܝܢ ܕܫܡܥܝܢ / ܠܗ ܐܠܐ ܡܛܠ ܚܘܒܗ ܕܡܫܝܚܐ ܡܦܝܣ ܐܢܐ / ܠܟܠܗܘܢ ܐܝܠܝܢ ܕܩܪ̈ܝܢ ܒܗ ܕܬܨܠܘܢ ܥܠ ܚܛܝܘܬܝ / ܐܝܬܝܪܐܝܬ ܠܟܝ [ܠܟcod.: ] ܡ‍[‍  ]ܐ ܡܦܝܣ ܐܢܐ ܕܠܐ ܬܛܥܝܢ / ܐܠܐ ܡܐ ܕܦܓܥ ܒܟ ܒܥܐ (؟) ܒܪܝܟܐ (؟) ܕ[  ] / ܬܬܥܗܕܝܢ [ܐܬܐܗܕܝܢcod.: ]  ܠܝ ܚܛܝܐ ܒܨܠܘܬܟܝ [ܒܨܠܘܬܟcod.: ] / || ܘܐܫܬܠܡ ܕܝܢ ܟܬܒܐ ܒܫܢܬ ܐܠܦ ܘܬܫܥ‍[ܝܢ ؟] / [ܕ]ܐܠܟܣܢܕܪܘܣ ܡܩܕ[ܘܢܝܐ] ܒܪ ܦܠ‍[‍ܝܦܘ]ܣ ܝܪܚ / ܬܡܘܙ [ܟܓ ܒܗ in margine:] ܒܝܘܡ ܬܠܬܐ [ܒ‍]‍ܫ‍[‍ܒ‍]‍ܐ ܒ‍[‍ܬܫ‍]‍ܥ [ܫܥܝ̈ܢ] / ܕܝܘܡܐ ܪ̈ܚܡܘܗܝ ܕܡܪܢ [ܝܫ‍]‍ܘܥ ܡܫܝܚܐ / ܢܗܘܘܢ ܥܠ ܚܛܝܐ ܕܟܬܒ ܟܬܒܐ ܗܢܐ ܐ[ܝܟ] / ܓܝܣܐ ܕܡܢ ܝܡܝܢܐ ܐܢ ܘܐܡܝܢ ܘܐܡܝܢ
translation: I, the wanting and sinful John, the stylite of Beth Mar QNWN the saint, in the city of Maʿarrat Meṣrēn, (in) the region of Antioch, [the city] of God, I copied this book for the benefit of myself and of my brethren and of those who will listen to it (the book). But for the love of Christ, I beseech all those, who will read it, to pray for my sinfulness, especially you, [       ], I beseech you not to forget (fem.), but whenever you encounter it (this book ?) (two uncertain words) [      ] to remember (fem.) me, a sinner, in your prayer./And the book was completed in the year one thousand and nine[teen ?] of Alexander the Macedonian, son of Philip, (in) the month of Tammuz (July), (on the day) 23 in it, on the third day in the week, at the ninth hour of the day. Let the mercy of Our Lord Jesus Christ be upon the sinner who copied this book, as (it was upon) the thief on the right hand, yea, and Amen, and Amen.

assoc_name.value: John the stylite of Beth Mar

note: Completed in the year one thousand and nine[teen ?] of Alexander the Macedonian, son of Philip, (in) the month of Tammuz (July), (on the day) 23 in it, on the third day in the week, at the ninth hour of the day

bib.citation: Lewis, Agnes Smith. Catalogue of the Syriac Manuscripts in the Convent of S. Catharine on Mount Sinai. Studia Sinaitica I. London: Cambridge University Press, 1894
Kāmil, Murād. Catalogue of All Manuscripts in the Monastery of St. Catherine on Mount Sinai. Harrassowitz, 1970
Brock, Sebastian P. “Syriac on Sinai: The Main Connections.” In Eukosmia: Studi miscellanei per il 75° di Vincenzo Poggi S.J. Edited by V. Ruggieri and L. Pieralli, 103-117. Catanzaro, 2003
Manuscripts in St. Catherine's Monastery, Mount Sinai. Library of Congress

program.label: NEH Syriac Parchment Descriptions Grant
program.description: Catalogued as part of UCLA Library's "Identification and Description of the Syriac and Arabic Manuscripts at St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, Phase 1: Syriac Parchment Manuscripts" project (2022-2025), funded by the National Endowment for the Humanities. Principle cataloguers were Grigory Kessel (Lead cataloguer), Natalia Smelova (Associate cataloguer), and Vevian Zaki (Cataloguer for Arabic content in Syriac manuscripts).

program.label: Sinai Palimpsests Project
program.description: Imaged and studied as part of the Sinai Palimpsests Project (2011–2016), a collaboration between St. Catherine’s Monastery of the Sinai, the Early Manuscripts Electronic Library, and UCLA Library, funded by the Arcadia Fund and the Ahmanson Foundation. The project used multispectral imaging and scholarly analysis to recover and document erased and obscured texts in palimpsest manuscripts from the monastery’s collection.
